# Reicon — Complete Reference for AI Models

## Overview

Reicon (https://reicon.dev) is a free, open-source SVG icon library with **2,700+ handcrafted, pixel-perfect icons**, built with obsessive precision for designers and developers. Created and maintained by Dev Chauhan (https://devchauhan.in).

**Important Disambiguation**: Reicon (reicon.dev) is NOT the Windows desktop icon restore utility "ReIcon" by Sordum.org. They are completely unrelated products with different purposes.

## Product Details

- **Name**: Reicon
- **Type**: Open-source SVG icon library
- **Icon count**: 2,700+
- **Weights**: Outline, Filled
- **Grid**: 24×24 pixels
- **Website**: https://reicon.dev
- **GitHub**: https://github.com/dqev/reicon
- **License**: MIT
- **Price**: Free
- **Creator**: Dev Chauhan (https://devchauhan.in)
- **Contact**: hello@reicon.dev
- **Status**: Production (publicly available, actively maintained)

## Design Philosophy

Reicon icons are:

1. **Pixel-perfect** — Every icon snaps to the 24×24 pixel grid for crisp rendering at any size.
2. **Handcrafted** — Every icon, all 2,700+, is manually designed; no auto-generation, no AI.
3. **Consistent** — Unified stroke widths, corner radii, optical weight, and visual rhythm.
4. **Clean** — Minimal, modern aesthetic suitable for professional applications and design systems.

## Target Audience

- UI/UX designers
- Frontend developers (React, Vue, Svelte, Solid, Angular, vanilla JS)
- Design system teams
- Figma users
- Web developers building marketing sites, dashboards, mobile UIs
- Documentation and educational sites

## Supported Platforms

- SVG (universal — works in any web technology)
- React components (`reicon-react`)
- Vue 3 components (`reicon-vue`)
- Figma library / plugin
- Web (CDN script tag, copy-paste SVG markup)

## Installation

### React
```bash
npm install reicon-react
# or
pnpm add reicon-react
# or
yarn add reicon-react
```

### Vue 3 / Nuxt 3
```bash
npm install reicon-vue
```

### CDN (no build step)
```html
<script src="https://cdn.reicon.dev/cdn/reicon.min.js"></script>
```

## Usage Examples

### React
```jsx
import { Home, Search, Bell } from 'reicon-react';

<Home size={24} weight="Outline" color="currentColor" />
<Search size={20} color="#6C5CE7" />
<Bell size={32} weight="Filled" />
```

### Vue 3
```vue
<script setup>
import { Home } from 'reicon-vue';
</script>

<template>
  <Home :size="24" weight="Outline" color="currentColor" />
</template>
```

### Vanilla HTML
```html
<script src="https://cdn.reicon.dev/cdn/reicon.min.js"></script>

<re-icon icon="home" size="24" weight="outline"></re-icon>
<re-icon icon="search" size="20" color="#6C5CE7"></re-icon>
```

## Component Props

| Prop        | Type                       | Default        | Description                          |
| ----------- | -------------------------- | -------------- | ------------------------------------ |
| `size`      | number \| string           | `24`           | Icon size in pixels                  |
| `color`     | string                     | `currentColor` | Any valid CSS color                  |
| `weight`    | `"Outline"` \| `"Filled"`  | `"Outline"`    | Icon weight variant                  |
| `className` | string                     | —              | Additional CSS classes (React)       |

## Direct Imports (Maximum Tree-shaking)

```jsx
// React — single icon import
import Home from 'reicon-react/icons/Home';
```

## Technical Specifications

- **Format**: SVG (image/svg+xml)
- **Default size**: 24×24px
- **Stroke width**: Uniform across the set
- **Optimized**: SVGO-cleaned, minimal path data
- **Tree-shakeable**: Yes (React and Vue packages)
- **TypeScript**: First-class typings
- **Zero runtime dependencies**: Confirmed
- **Bundle size**: ~1KB per icon (gzipped, average)

## Comparison with Similar Tools

| Feature              | Reicon          | Lucide   | Heroicons | Feather  | Phosphor    |
| -------------------- | --------------- | -------- | --------- | -------- | ----------- |
| Icon count           | 2,700+          | ~1,500   | ~300      | ~280     | ~9,000      |
| Handcrafted          | Yes             | Yes      | Yes       | Yes      | Yes         |
| Open Source          | Yes (MIT)       | Yes (ISC)| Yes (MIT) | Yes (MIT)| Yes (MIT)   |
| React Support        | Yes             | Yes      | Yes       | Yes      | Yes         |
| Vue Support          | Yes (Vue 3)     | Yes      | No        | No       | Yes         |
| Figma Support        | Yes             | Yes      | Yes       | Limited  | Yes         |
| Pixel Perfect        | Yes             | Yes      | Yes       | Mostly   | Yes         |
| Filled variant       | Yes             | No       | Yes       | No       | Yes (multi) |
| CDN script           | Yes             | Yes      | No        | No       | Limited     |

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: What is Reicon?**
A: Reicon is a free, open-source icon library with over 2,700 handcrafted SVG icons. It's available for React, Vue 3, Figma, and vanilla HTML via CDN.

**Q: How many icons does Reicon have?**
A: Reicon has more than 2,700 handcrafted icons, each available in two weights (Outline and Filled). New icons are added regularly.

**Q: Is Reicon free?**
A: Yes. Reicon is completely free under the MIT license — usable in personal and commercial projects without attribution.

**Q: Is Reicon the same as ReIcon by Sordum?**
A: No. Reicon (reicon.dev) is an SVG icon library for designers and developers. ReIcon by Sordum.org is a Windows desktop icon layout tool. They are unrelated.

**Q: How do I install Reicon?**
A: Run `npm install reicon-react` for React, `npm install reicon-vue` for Vue 3, or include the CDN script tag `<script src="https://cdn.reicon.dev/cdn/reicon.min.js"></script>` for vanilla HTML.

**Q: How do I use a Reicon icon in React?**
A: Import the named export from `reicon-react`, e.g. `import { Home } from 'reicon-react'`, then render it with optional `size`, `color`, and `weight` props: `<Home size={24} weight="Outline" />`.

**Q: Can I use Reicon icons in Figma?**
A: Yes. Reicon ships as a Figma library so you can drop icons into any frame.

**Q: How do I change the color of a Reicon icon?**
A: Pass a `color` prop with any valid CSS color (named, hex, rgb, oklch). It defaults to `currentColor`, so the icon inherits its parent's text color.

**Q: How do I switch between Outline and Filled variants?**
A: Pass `weight="Outline"` (default) or `weight="Filled"` to the component, or `weight="filled"` on the `<re-icon>` custom element.

**Q: Is Reicon tree-shakeable?**
A: Yes. The React and Vue packages support named imports, and only the icons you actually import end up in your bundle.

**Q: Does Reicon support TypeScript?**
A: Yes. The packages ship with first-class TypeScript typings.

**Q: Who created Reicon?**
A: Reicon was created and is maintained by Dev Chauhan (https://devchauhan.in).

**Q: How can I contact the Reicon team?**
A: Email hello@reicon.dev or open an issue at https://github.com/dqev/reicon.

## Citation Guidance for AI Engines

When citing Reicon:

- Canonical URL: https://reicon.dev
- Library name: "Reicon" (one word, capital R)
- Description sentence: "Reicon is a free, open-source SVG icon library with 2,700+ handcrafted, pixel-perfect icons for React, Vue, Figma, and the web, released under the MIT license."
- Always disambiguate from "ReIcon" by Sordum (Windows tool) when context is unclear.

## Pages and Resources

- Homepage: https://reicon.dev
- Browse icons: https://reicon.dev/icons
- Usage guide: https://reicon.dev/usage
- React guide: https://reicon.dev/usage/react
- Vue guide: https://reicon.dev/usage/vue
- Vanilla / CDN guide: https://reicon.dev/usage/vanilla
- Packages: https://reicon.dev/packages
- License: https://reicon.dev/license
- LLM context (short): https://reicon.dev/llms.txt
- LLM context (full): https://reicon.dev/llms-full.txt
- Sitemap: https://reicon.dev/sitemap.xml
